background.inspiredBy: Seedlip’s restraint and Lyre’s premium hosting cues support a soft, adult base that keeps the system from becoming cheap party clutter.
background.name: Champagne Cream
background.role: main light background for website sections, venue cards, can negative space and premium product storytelling

background.inspiredBy: Kin Euphorics and Poppi show how soft tinted worlds can feel lifestyle-led; this version is warmer and more social, avoiding wellness-spa quietness.
background.name: Peach Glow
background.role: secondary warm background for social posts, flavour panels and event-led moments

background.inspiredBy: Lyre’s and Seedlip bring evening hospitality credibility; the deep tone adds bar/night-out sophistication without imitating formal spirits.
background.name: After-Dark Grape
background.role: dark background for night-mode hero sections, reversed lockups, premium menus and high-contrast story ads

competitorInspiration: Tier 1 points PEP toward adult, occasion-led colour: Kin Euphorics’ emotional warmth, Lyre’s hospitality polish and Seedlip’s restraint keep the brand premium rather than gym-led. Tier 2 adds permission for fruit-forward energy and ready-to-serve vibrancy from Ole cocktails, Poppi and Yaya Kombucha, while avoiding aperitif subtlety or fermentation craft heaviness. Tier 3 clarifies what not to do: no clinical electrolyte blues, no performance black-and-neon, no nutrition-first supplement palette and no childish juice-box sweetness.

primary.inspiredBy: Kin Euphorics’ mood-led warmth and Ole cocktails’ festive can energy confirm a confident social colour that still feels adult.
primary.name: Spritz Coral
primary.role: primary brand colour — wordmark, key borders, hero shapes, flavour-led headlines and main CTA buttons

primary.inspiredBy: Lyre’s and Seedlip inform the need for bar-credible depth; this gives PEP a premium evening anchor without copying spirits heritage codes.
primary.name: Velvet Plum
primary.role: premium anchor — dark panels, can top/bottom bands, navigation, wordmark reversal and hospitality materials

primary.inspiredBy: Ole cocktails and EVER TREE support a bright serving-ritual accent; the golden fruit note keeps the system celebratory, not clinical.
primary.name: Mango Spark
primary.role: celebration accent — sparkle motifs, flavour highlights, badge fills and small moments of festival energy

rejectPatterns: Clinical blue-and-white hydration palettes that imply utility, replenishment or medical proof.
Black, chrome and neon gym-performance palettes that make protein feel like bodybuilding fuel.
Overly beige wellness-spa palettes that remove the social, fruity, night-out energy.
Heavy black-and-gold spirits imitation that makes PEP feel like a formal alcohol substitute rather than its own drink.
Primary-colour rainbow clutter or cartoon juice tones that make the brand feel cheap or childlike.

secondary.inspiredBy: Kin Euphorics and Recess show the value of gentle lifestyle tints; PEP uses this as a warmer, more outgoing social wash.
secondary.name: Lychee Blush
secondary.role: soft support colour — flavour backgrounds, quote cards, table-card backs and calm contrast against Spritz Coral

secondary.inspiredBy: Ole cocktails’ ready-to-party warmth and fruit-forward RTD cues support this energetic but controlled orange accent.
secondary.name: Aperitivo Orange
secondary.role: warm accent — secondary CTAs, fruit illustrations, can side stripes and motion graphics

secondary.inspiredBy: Poppi and Kin Euphorics validate expressive lifestyle colour; the raspberry tone adds grown-up playfulness without kids’ juice softness.
secondary.name: Raspberry Pulse
secondary.role: playful accent — social stickers, limited flavour details, event graphics and small highlight typography

text.inspiredBy: Seedlip and Lyre’s use depth and restraint for adult credibility; this replaces supplement black with a warmer hospitality dark.
text.name: Midnight Ink
text.role: primary text colour for headlines, body copy and navigation on light backgrounds

text.inspiredBy: EVER TREE and Yaya Kombucha show that softer dark text can feel premium and crafted; useful for secondary reading without harshness.
text.name: Soft Fig
text.role: secondary text for captions, ingredient notes, product facts and long-form copy

text.inspiredBy: Premium alcohol-free brands often reverse cleanly on rich dark grounds; this warm off-white keeps dark layouts inviting.
text.name: Cream Foam
text.role: reversed text on Velvet Plum and After-Dark Grape backgrounds
